pattern: Forcing every length-k circular window to share the same sum collapses into forcing arr[i] to equal arr[i+k] (wrapping) for every i, which splits the array into gcd(n,k) independent groups that each must become one constant value chosen to minimize total absolute movement -- the group's median, not its mean.
hints: Write out what 'every length-k subarray sums the same' actually implies about arr[i] versus arr[i+k] (with wraparound) for a single step i -> i+1, then follow that chain forward from any starting index.

That chain of forced equalities loops back on itself after n / gcd(n, k) steps, so it partitions all indices into exactly gcd(n, k) independent groups, each of which must collapse to one shared value.
For one group, the total number of +1/-1 operations needed to make every element equal is minimized by picking the group's median as the shared value, not its average.

edge_inputs.description: single element, k equal to the array length
k does not evenly divide n, so gcd(n,k) is 1 and every element joins one group
k equal to n, so every element is already its own group
all elements already identical, zero operations needed
k = 1 forces the entire circular array into a single group, not just neighboring pairs
gcd(n,k) greater than 1, so the array splits into interleaved groups rather than one block per k elements
two elements at opposite ends of the value range, forced into one group

stress.description: random array sizes with a random k, exercising many different gcd(n,k) group structures
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(2, min(size, 4000))
    k = rng.randint(1, n)
    arr = [rng.randint(1, 10**9) for _ in range(n)]
    return [arr, k]

solutions.python:
import math
class Solution:
    def makeSubKSumEqual(self, arr, k):
        n = len(arr)
        g = math.gcd(n, k)
        total = 0
        for r in range(g):
            group = sorted(arr[r::g])
            median = group[len(group) // 2]
            total += sum(abs(x - median) for x in group)
        return total
